notes: Stainless steel provides excellent resistance to liquid bulk cargoes including chemicals and vegetable oils.
Standard epoxy coatings are suitable for many liquid bulk cargoes but may degrade with aggressive solvents.

flags: Zinc silicate reacts with acidic liquid cargoes
notes: Zinc silicate is not recommended for liquid bulk due to potential chemical reaction with acidic or alkaline cargoes.
Phenolic epoxy offers superior chemical resistance, ideal for aggressive liquid cargoes.
Stainless steel holds are suitable for dry bulk but represent overspecification for non-corrosive cargoes.
Epoxy coatings provide good abrasion resistance for dry bulk cargoes like grain and minerals.

flags: Monitor for abrasion wear on zinc silicate
notes: Zinc silicate is acceptable for dry bulk but less abrasion-resistant than epoxy for heavy granular cargoes.
Phenolic epoxy works well for dry bulk, though its chemical resistance advantages are underutilized.
Stainless steel holds are suitable for containerized cargo. No direct cargo-coating contact expected.
Epoxy is the standard coating for container vessel holds. Excellent durability and cost-effectiveness.
Zinc silicate provides adequate protection for container holds with minimal direct cargo contact.
Phenolic epoxy is acceptable but overspecified for standard containerized cargo.
Stainless steel is suitable for break bulk but may sustain surface marks from heavy lifts.
Epoxy provides good impact resistance for break bulk operations including steel coils and project cargo.

flags: Risk of coating damage from heavy break bulk items
notes: Zinc silicate may chip under heavy break bulk loading. Additional dunnage recommended.
Phenolic epoxy provides adequate protection for break bulk cargo operations.

flags: Stainless steel decks may be slippery for wheeled cargo
notes: Stainless steel is not standard for RoRo operations. Anti-slip treatment may be required.
Epoxy with anti-slip aggregate is the standard coating for RoRo decks and ramps.

flags: Zinc silicate cannot withstand wheeled traffic abrasion
notes: Zinc silicate is not suitable for RoRo operations due to rapid coating degradation from vehicle traffic.
Phenolic epoxy offers good abrasion resistance for RoRo operations but is less cost-effective than standard epoxy.
Stainless steel is ideal for refrigerated cargo holds, withstanding thermal cycling and condensation.

flags: Monitor epoxy integrity under thermal cycling
notes: Standard epoxy may develop micro-cracks under repeated thermal cycling in reefer holds.

flags: Zinc silicate degrades under condensation and thermal stress
notes: Zinc silicate fails under refrigerated conditions due to moisture condensation and thermal expansion mismatch.
Phenolic epoxy performs well in refrigerated holds with good condensation resistance.
Stainless steel tanks are the standard for LPG/LNG carriers. Excellent cryogenic properties.
Standard epoxy coatings become brittle and fail at cryogenic temperatures required for gas cargo.

flags: Zinc silicate fails at cryogenic temperatures
notes: Zinc silicate is completely unsuitable for gas carrier tanks due to cryogenic brittleness.

flags: Phenolic epoxy not rated for cryogenic service
notes: Phenolic epoxy lacks cryogenic certification required for LPG/LNG containment systems.
